mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in real retail sales captures contemporaneous household consumption momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because equity prices reflect near-term earnings expectations tied to consumer spending.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly growth fails to positively correlate with 20-day forward returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment jointly signal labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither variable alone suffices.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery periods, accelerating industrial production signals capacity utilization improvements and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In post-crisis recovery regimes, INDPRO accelerations fail to correlate with positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.04) or the hit ratio of positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the CPI index surprise markets and raise near-term inflation risk premia, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as discount rates adjust rapidly.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or Rank IC is not negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, the labor market is deteriorating from a tight state, triggering downward revisions to corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of trigger events, or the mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising retail sales jointly confirm broad-based demand expansion that boosts cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to generate positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (t-test p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below its 3-month rolling average signals contracting manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines below its 3-month average are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC is above -0.03 over a 2-year window.
